title: Albertian Channel Memory in Black-Hole Evaporation
abstract: The AMPS paradox assumes a globally associative tensor-product stage for the early radiation, the exterior Hawking mode, and the interior partner. We study a retained attractor sector of octonionic magical supergravity whose horizon symbols form the Albert algebra J3(O). This induces an Albertian algebraic-quantum description: states are positive normalized functionals, events are Jordan idempotents, reversible motions are algebra automorphisms, and ordinary quantum mechanics is recovered on associative readout blocks. Peirce theory then splits the horizon data into a hidden exceptional complement, an interface relay, and a two-helicity exterior detector. Eliminating the relay gives a source-fixed Volterra memory law on a neutral-source fixed-charge Reissner--Nordstrom evaporation trajectory. In real time, the leading one-time occupation follows the sourced evaporation clock, while the retained-memory imprint appears as a spectral-overlap connected two-time coherence of windowed helicity/Stokes observables in the emitted history. In Euclidean time, the Peirce--Volterra kernel becomes a transfer kernel with two branchwise superstatistical limits: a regular-opening Tsallis/Lomax onset and a near-extremal shifted-Levy residence branch. The lower admissible envelope of the endpoint actions then reconstructs the Page-curve envelope. The result is an ordinary emitted readout with exceptional memory, not a restored AMPS tensor factorization.
summary: This paper proposes that black hole evaporation does not require a return to standard associative tensor-product factorization to resolve the AMPS paradox. Instead, it suggests that the evaporation process possesses an 'exceptional memory' derived from octonionic supergravity. This memory-driven approach allows for the reconstruction of the Page curve through a non-associative algebraic framework.
main_problem: The paper addresses the AMPS paradox regarding the entanglement structure and tensor-product factorization of Hawking radiation, early radiation, and interior partners during black hole evaporation.
main_result: The study proposes that black hole evaporation follows an Albertian algebraic-quantum description where an exceptional memory law (Volterra type) replaces the need for restored tensor factorization, successfully reconstructing the Page curve.
method: The author utilizes the framework of octonionic magical supergravity and Peirce theory applied to the Albert algebra J3(O) to derive a memory-dependent evolution of the horizon data.
model_or_system: The system is a Reissner-Nordstrom black hole undergoing evaporation, modeled via an attractor sector of octonionic magical supergravity where horizon symbols form the Albert algebra.
key_observables: windowed helicity/Stokes observables, spectral-overlap connected two-time coherence, and the Page-curve envelope.
important_parameters: Reissner-Nordstrom evaporation trajectory, Euclidean time limits (Tsallis/Lomax onset and shifted-Levy residence branch), and the near-extremal limit.
main_assumptions: The paper assumes a retained attractor sector of octonionic magical supergravity and uses the elimination of an 'interface relay' to derive the Volterra memory law.
paper_structure: The paper moves from the critique of the AMPS paradox to the introduction of the Albert algebra framework, followed by the application of Peirce theory to split horizon data, the derivation of the Volterra memory law in real and Euclidean time, and finally the reconstruction of the Page curve.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
